# .github/workflows/build.yml

name: Build and Run Hello World   # Name shown in GitHub Actions tab

on:
  push:                           # Trigger when you push code
    branches:
      - main
  pull_request:                   # Also trigger on pull requests
    branches:
      - main

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest        # Use a Linux machine on GitHub's servers

    steps:
      # Step 1: Download your code onto the GitHub machine
      - name: Checkout Code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      # Step 2: Install .NET on the GitHub machine
      - name: Setup .NET
        uses: actions/setup-dotnet@v4
        with:
          dotnet-version: '8.0.x'

      # Step 3: Restore dependencies (downloads any packages needed)
      - name: Restore Dependencies
        run: dotnet restore HelloWorldApp/HelloWorldApp.csproj

      # Step 4: Build the project (compile the code)
      - name: Build
        run: dotnet build HelloWorldApp/HelloWorldApp.csproj --configuration Release --no-restore

      # Step 5: Run the app and see "Hello, World!" in the logs
      - name: Run
        run: dotnet run --project HelloWorldApp/HelloWorldApp.csproj
